recent_excerpts: The Kyber ransomware gang is using a variant that implements Kyber1024 post-quantum encryption to target Windows and VMware ESXi systems, according to a BleepingComputer analysis.
Kyber ransomware deploys two distinct payloads to encrypt both Windows systems and VMware ESXi servers, using a custom tool to wipe ESXi snapshots and hinder recovery.
PureRAT hides its Windows PE payloads inside PNG files and executes them filelessly in memory, a technique detailed by cybersecurity researchers analyzing a new sophisticated campaign.
